Man in black shirt.description: Wearing a black athletic shirt and blue reflective sunglasses, running along a desert path and later a paved road. He speaks frequently to the camera and interacts with the Man in blue shirt, possessing a distinct Belgian/European accent. He discusses his lack of sleep from a DJ set and early flight, his physical state during the run, questions their route, comments on the desert landscape, and expresses excitement for the training week, noting the beauty compared to Belgium.

Airport terminal.description: A modern, well-lit airport terminal with check-in counters, signage, and travelers, identified as Brussels Airport on May 14th, 2023.

Man in white and green jersey.description: Wearing a white and green athletic jersey with a red logo, standing in a rocky desert and stating in a male voice, 'And I'm so dehydrated.'

Iberia airplane.description: A white and red Iberia aircraft with a bicycle graphic on the fuselage, shown on the tarmac and later inside with passengers boarding.

Cyclist in green jersey.description: Wearing a green cycling jersey, white helmet, and reflective sunglasses, riding through a desert landscape, exclaiming excitedly 'I went above 90!' and later heard complaining about 'working the fucking wind' in a frustrated male voice.

Man in blue shirt.description: Wearing a blue Garmin-branded athletic shirt, black cap, and white earbuds, running through the desert and later on a paved road, holding a phone and checking his route. He speaks with a similar Belgian/European accent, discussing the start of 'Hell Week' training, his fatigue from lack of sleep, confirming their route (with some doubt), admitting they might be lost, commenting on the cold wind, and describing his physical state as he loosens up in the 'weird terrain' of the desert.

Cyclist in red jersey.description: Wearing a red and white cycling jersey, white helmet, and sunglasses, sitting on a bike and expressing frustration with a male voice saying, 'I don't wanna train.'

Man in airport (white shirt).description: Wearing a white long-sleeve shirt, black cap, and carrying a backpack and suitcase, later seen shirtless in the apartment with a visible side tattoo, wearing light-colored shorts. He has an excited voice, exclaiming 'Oh my God! It's my bike!' and later gives a friendly 'house tour' of the apartment, describing 'Arno's closet,' 'my closet,' 'our living room,' and introducing 'our god, the one and only, Nocafé,' before mentioning 'Arno's bike, my bike' on the rooftop.

Swimmer in wetsuit.description: Wearing a black wetsuit and yellow swim cap, entering a pool while being coached aggressively.

Man in airport (black shirt).description: Wearing a black t-shirt, black cap, and carrying a backpack and suitcase, later seen in a black Nike shirt with sunglasses perched on his head, interacting animatedly in the apartment and on the rooftop. He has a cheerful voice, mentioning 'Gate number two' and 'Happy hour, no?' and laughing warmly in the apartment.

Coach in wetsuit.description: Wearing a black wetsuit and swim cap, standing in the pool and yelling aggressively at the swimmer, 'Stop fucking around now, get swimming!'

Blue bicycle.description: A blue road bicycle with 'DT SWISS' written on the wheel, visible inside the apartment and later on the rooftop terrace, identified by the Man in airport (white shirt) as 'Arno's bike, my bike'.

Apartment interior.description: A small, modern apartment with a kitchen, dining table, bedroom, and closet, featuring a blue curtain, white pendant light, and a photo taped to the wall.

Rooftop terrace.description: A sunny outdoor space with a wooden table, wicker chairs, and large glass doors, where the two men sit and discuss their training plans, accompanied by the sounds of paper rustling and their conversation.

Desert running path.description: A gravel path in a dry, rocky desert landscape under a clear blue sky, where the Man in black shirt runs during the first day of training, accompanied by upbeat electronic music.

raw_segments: The video opens with two men walking outdoors in a sunny, arid environment with palm trees and stone walls, as upbeat, driving electronic music begins. One man, in a black shirt, states their goal to "train like a pro triathlete for six days." The scene quickly shifts to a cyclist in a green jersey and white helmet riding through a desert landscape, exclaiming excitedly, "I went above 90!" accompanied by a whoosh sound. This is followed by a swimmer in a wetsuit and swim cap entering a pool, as a coach aggressively yells, "Stop fucking around now, get swimming!" The electronic music continues with a vocal sample. The narrative then cuts to a man in a white and green jersey, visibly dehydrated, standing in a rocky desert, stating, "And I'm so dehydrated." This is followed by a close-up of a cyclist in a red jersey and white helmet, frustrated and holding his head, groaning and saying, "I don't wanna train." Another man, in a green jersey and black helmet, complains about "working the fucking wind." The electronic music continues as a voice declares, "Welcome to the real world," and another adds, "The wind is fucked up," before the music fades slightly. The scene returns to the two men walking outdoors, now with the man in the black shirt speaking animatedly to the camera, gesturing with his hands, introducing the video from Lanzarote, which he describes as "the Mecca for triathletes worldwide," explaining they are there for a training camp before an Ironman, as advised by "beloved Uncle Freek." He continues, detailing their journey "all the way from Brussels to Lanzarote to train like a pro triathlete for six days." Upbeat Latin-infused music with Spanish singing then begins, and a voice welcomes viewers to a "new video." The setting changes to an airport terminal on May 14th, 2023, where the two men, now in casual travel attire, are pulling suitcases. The man in the black shirt announces it's "14th of May, 6 o'clock in the morning," as they head to "drop off our bikes." The final shot shows them boarding an Iberia airplane, with one man smiling at the camera as they settle into their seats, accompanied by the continuing Latin music.

The video opens with the Man in airport (white shirt) and the Man in airport (black shirt) exiting an Iberia airplane at Madrid airport. Upbeat, energetic music plays as the Man in airport (white shirt) excitedly exclaims, "Just arrived in Madrid! Oh my God! Oh my God! It's my bike! Oh my God!" upon spotting his bicycle on the tarmac. The scene transitions to the Iberia airplane's interior with passengers boarding, then a surreal composite shot of a plane flying low over a beach. The upbeat music continues as the Man in airport (black shirt) mentions "Gate number two" and "Happy hour, no?". The two men are then seen arriving in Lanzarote, walking across the tarmac under a bright, windy sky, with a male voice noting, "Very windy, Lanzarote." The music transitions to a more relaxed, acoustic guitar tune as they enter a modest apartment. Inside, the Man in airport (white shirt) is shirtless, unpacking, and revealing a side tattoo. He gestures around, giving a friendly "house tour," stating, "Hi everybody, welcome to this house tour. So, this is where we sleep. I sleep here, Arno sleeps here," and pointing out "Arno's closet here." He then opens the door to greet the Man in airport (black shirt), who enters wearing a black Nike shirt and sunglasses. The Man in airport (white shirt) exclaims "Oh, Arno!" with a laugh, and the Man in airport (black shirt) responds with laughter. The Man in airport (white shirt) asks "Why are you so surprised?" before showing "This is my closet." The Man in airport (black shirt) points to a photo on the wall as the Man in airport (white shirt) describes "This is our living room," and then introduces "our god, the one and only, Nocafé." The Man in airport (black shirt) then moves toward the kitchen area, where a blue bicycle is visible. The segment concludes on a sunny rooftop terrace, with the Man in airport (black shirt) smiling and gesturing. The Man in airport (white shirt) explains, "Voilà, so we already unpacked a little. So this is Arno's bike, my bike," as the sound of wind is heard, indicating they are preparing for their next activity.

The video opens on a sunny rooftop terrace where the Man in black shirt, wearing a black athletic shirt with sunglasses perched on his head, animatedly reads from a piece of paper, his voice clear and determined, detailing their training plans. The Man in blue shirt, in a blue Garmin-branded shirt, listens intently, occasionally gesturing or reacting with surprise. Sounds of paper rustling accompany the Man in black shirt's speech as he reads about becoming an 'Iron Man' and a training schedule from 'Fre,' including an inspirational quote: 'that triathlon is not going to train for itself. So we go on a training camp.' He then outlines their first day's plan: an 8-kilometer run, followed by a recovery shake, water, dinner, and early bed, emphasizing that tomorrow will be 'double training.' After a distinct sound of a high-five or clap, the scene cuts to a first-person perspective of the Man in black shirt running on a gravel path in a dry, rocky desert landscape under a bright blue sky. Upbeat electronic music begins as on-screen text announces 'DAY 1 TRAINING 1 8KM - RUNNING'. The Man in black shirt, now running, speaks to the camera with determination, confirming it's their 'first training day in Lanzarote.'

The video opens with the Man in black shirt, wearing a black athletic shirt and blue reflective sunglasses, running along a gravel path in a dry, rocky desert landscape under a clear blue sky. He speaks directly to the camera in a Belgian/European accent, explaining he's running on little sleep after a DJ set and an early flight, but feels good. The scene shifts to the Man in blue shirt, wearing a blue Garmin-branded athletic shirt, black cap, and white earbuds, also running through the desert with the sun low on the horizon. He shares that this is the 'first kilometer in' of 'Hell Week' training, feeling heavy from lack of sleep but looking forward to the week. The two men briefly interact; the Man in black shirt asks, 'Are you sure this is the way?', to which the Man in blue shirt replies, 'I think so, yeah.' The Man in black shirt then comments, 'It looks like the middle of a desert,' prompting the Man in blue shirt to respond, 'Okay, we might be a little bit lost.' They continue running, with the Man in black shirt checking in, 'All good, still feeling it?' and receiving an affirmative reply. He then remarks on the beautiful scenery, noting it's 'definitely different than training in rainy Belgium,' and expresses feeling well despite the lack of sleep, excited for the week. As the environment transitions and they approach a paved road with parked cars and traffic signs, the Man in blue shirt comments on 'a cold wind.' The Man in black shirt asks, 'How's it going?', and the Man in blue shirt replies, 'Good,' adding that he was 'really stiff in the beginning' but is 'loosening up' in this 'weird terrain' of the desert. The Man in black shirt runs across a crosswalk, smiling and gesturing to the camera, while the Man in blue shirt follows, still focused on his phone, as the setting becomes more urban.

event_blocks.summary: The Man in black shirt runs at sunset, transitioning to a rocky desert path, visibly sweating and wearing blue reflective sunglasses, stating "you're almost in the city" and confirming "8K done. Little warm-up for what's coming this week. Actually feels really good," displaying an 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 117' Strava summary for an 8.01 km run at 5:37/km pace. The Man in blue shirt also runs, checks his phone, and shows an 'Evening Run' Strava summary of 8.04 km at 6:26/km pace. After heavy breathing and running sounds, both men return to their apartment. On the sunny rooftop terrace, the Man in blue shirt, now shirtless with a heart rate monitor, greets the Man in black shirt, who prepares a protein shake, calling it "choco milk." The Man in blue shirt asks "How was it?" and declares "And I'm done," discussing plans for a "shower, go get some food, then go to bed." The Man in black shirt notes, "Easiest training of the whole week... prepare for what's coming tomorrow," followed by playful banter. The video ends with a view of a messy apartment bedroom and a blue curtain, cutting to a title card 'DAY 2' with calm piano and strings music. The next minute opens in a dimly lit bedroom with rhythmic music, showing the Man in white shirt and Man in black shirt asleep. As light brightens, the Man in white shirt groans awake, stretches, and sits up, revealing his bare torso and left arm tattoo. He picks up a document labeled 'DAY 3', referring to it as 'Day two', and reads, 'triathletes eat and train, they don't diet and exercise,' outlining a 3-kilometer swim, lunch, and a 60-kilometer bike ride. He then asks the still-sleeping Man in black shirt if he's ready. In a bright, modern dining area, the Man in white shirt eats porridge with peanut butter, pancakes with yogurt and chocolate, a banana, and orange juice. The Man in black shirt, wearing a black Nike shirt with sunglasses on his head, describes his breakfast of scrambled eggs ('huevo'), bread, turkey, 'fancy meat,' and beans in tomato sauce, also with orange juice. The Man in white shirt comments, 'Nice. Healthy,' as they eat their nutritious meal with continuing background music.

Man in black shirt.description: Wearing a black athletic shirt and blue reflective sunglasses, running along a desert path and later a paved road, speaking to the camera with a clear, articulate voice about his 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 117' run, describing it as '8K done' and a 'little warm-up' that 'feels really good.' He later prepares and drinks a protein shake, describing it as 'choco milk,' and discusses his training plan and post-run routine with the Man in blue shirt, engaging in playful banter.

Man in blue shirt.description: Wearing a blue Garmin-branded athletic shirt, black cap, and white earbuds while running through the desert and later on a paved road, holding a phone and checking his route. He later appears shirtless with a heart rate monitor, sitting on the rooftop terrace, checking his watch and phone, smiling and gesturing. He speaks with a clear, conversational voice, asking about the other man's run, declaring his own run 'done,' and discussing post-run routines like 'shower, go get some food, then go to bed' with a slightly playful tone.

Apartment interior.description: A small, modern apartment with a kitchen, dining table, bedroom, and closet, featuring a blue curtain, white pendant light, and a photo taped to the wall.

Rooftop terrace.description: A sunny outdoor space with a wooden table, wicker chairs, and large glass doors, where the two men sit and discuss their training plans.

Dining area.description: A bright, communal space with white tables, teal placemats, large windows, and other people in the background, suggesting a shared training facility or hostel.

Document 'DAY 3'.description: A printed training schedule, visually labeled 'DAY 3', is read aloud by the Man in white shirt, who refers to it as 'Day two'. It includes the quote: 'triathletes eat and train, they don't diet and exercise,' and outlines the day's activities: a 3-kilometer swim, lunch, and a 60-kilometer bike ride.

Breakfast spread.description: The Man in white shirt's breakfast spread includes porridge with peanut butter, pancakes with yogurt and chocolate, a banana, and orange juice, which he verbally details. The Man in black shirt's breakfast, which he describes, consists of scrambled eggs ('huevo'), bread, turkey, 'fancy meat,' and beans in tomato sauce, also accompanied by orange juice.

Man in airport (white shirt).description: Initially seen shirtless and wrapped in a white duvet, with curly dark hair and a visible tattoo on his left arm, he wakes with a deep groan, then speaks in a calm, slightly accented voice, reading the day's schedule and later describing his breakfast of porridge, peanut butter, pancakes, yogurt, chia, and chocolate. He is later seen eating this meal at a dining table.

Man in airport (black shirt).description: Initially asleep in bed, he later wakes and is seen eating breakfast at the dining table, wearing a black Nike shirt with sunglasses perched on his head. He responds with a muffled 'Almost' when asked if he's ready, and describes his breakfast as 'huevo, bread, turkey, fancy meat, beans in tomato sauce.'

He speaks to the camera, saying "you're almost in the city" and later confirming "8K done. Little warm-up for what's coming this week. Actually feels really good," while displaying a Strava workout summary for 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 117' showing an 8.01 km run at 5:37/km pace. The scene shifts to the Man in blue shirt, who is also running, checking his phone, and later shown with a Strava summary for an 'Evening Run' of 8.04 km at 6:26/km pace. After sounds of heavy breathing and running, both men return to their apartment. The Man in blue shirt, now shirtless and wearing a heart rate monitor, greets the Man in black shirt, who is still in his blue Garmin shirt. They sit on the sunny rooftop terrace, where the Man in black shirt prepares a protein shake with distinct shaking sounds, describing it as "choco milk." They engage in conversation, with the Man in blue shirt asking "How was it?" and later declaring "And I'm done," before discussing post-run plans like "shower, go get some food, then go to bed." He picks up a printed document, visually labeled 'DAY 3', and in a calm, slightly accented voice, reads from it, referring to it as 'Day two'. He then gestures towards the still-sleeping Man in black shirt before asking if he's ready. The scene transitions to a bright, modern communal dining area with large windows and other people, where the Man in white shirt is now seated at a table with his breakfast, which he describes as porridge with peanut butter, pancakes with yogurt and chocolate, a banana, and orange juice. The Man in black shirt, now awake and wearing a black Nike shirt with sunglasses on his head, responds with a muffled 'Almost' and describes his own breakfast of scrambled eggs ('huevo'), bread, turkey, 'fancy meat,' and beans in tomato sauce, also with orange juice. The Man in white shirt comments, 'Nice. Healthy,' as the two begin their day with a nutritious meal, the background music continuing throughout.

event_blocks.summary: On a sunny beach with calm water, a man in a white t-shirt printed with 'average' enthusiastically declared, "Beautiful day, let's go!" He then discussed swimming in the lagoon with a man in a black t-shirt, who stated, "We're swimming in the lagoon today." The man in white described the water temperature as "average" and mentioned they would be coached for open water swimming by a professional triathlete. Sam Laidlow, identified by a social media overlay as an athlete and Kona bike course record holder, arrived and was introduced by the man in white, who asked the group to "make some noise for Sam Laidlow!" Sam greeted them with a hug, joking, "Try to make you guys suffer!" Later, the man in white and green jersey and Sam Laidlow crouched by the clear water, discussing Iron Man times, with the man in white stating his times as "7:42, 7:43" and recounting breaking a world record by "eight or nine minutes" but not winning against "Gustav." Sam exclaimed, "Motherfucker." As they walked, the man in black shirt joined them, commenting on the "very salty" water and humorously calling the man in white and green jersey "the ghetto version of Sam Laidlow," to which the man in white and green jersey responded by calling himself "the little fat brother." The conversation turned to attire, with jokes about swimming "naked" under wetsuits and the suggestion, "Let's all get naked now." The man in white and green jersey smiled at the camera before walking away, while Sam and the man in black shirt continued their conversation.

Beach setting.description: A sandy beach area adjacent to calm water, bordered by a stone wall, with palm trees and a building labeled 'Club Laganta' in the background; bright, sunny, and open, suggesting a coastal training or recreational location.

Sam Laidlow.description: Wearing a black t-shirt, purple shorts, and a pink cap; has a beard and curly hair; seen crouching by the water, gesturing, laughing, and conversing. Audibly, he speaks with a clear male voice, asking about Iron Man times, exclaiming 'Gustav' and 'Motherfucker' in response to a competitor, and asking about wearing a tri suit or just trunks. He is identified as an athlete and Kona bike course record holder.

Man in black shirt.description: Wearing a black t-shirt with 'To The Atmosphere' printed on it, green patterned shorts, and a watch on his left wrist; has curly hair; seen laughing and joining the conversation. Audibly, he speaks with a clear male voice, commenting that the 'water is very salty,' humorously calling the Man in white and green jersey 'the ghetto version of Sam Laidlow,' and suggesting 'let's all get naked now.'

Man in white and green jersey.description: Wearing a white t-shirt with 'average' printed on it, black shorts, and a watch on his left wrist; has curly dark hair and a mustache; seen smiling, laughing, and interacting warmly. Audibly, he speaks with a clear male voice, discussing his Iron Man time (7:42/7:43), breaking a world record by 8-9 minutes but still losing, training with his dad, and humorously referring to himself as the 'little fat brother.' He also jokes about swimming 'naked' and getting 'in prison' for it, and not bringing his trunks.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a man in a white t-shirt with 'average' printed on it, smiling under a large white umbrella, as he enthusiastically declares, "Beautiful day, let's go!" The scene quickly transitions to a bright, sunny beach near calm water, bordered by a stone wall and palm trees, where the same man, with a clear and enthusiastic voice, interacts with another man in a black t-shirt and green patterned shorts. They discuss swimming in the lagoon, with the man in the black shirt mentioning, "We're swimming in the lagoon today," and the man in white adding, "Not hot, but uh... average!" followed by laughter and applause. The man in white then explains they'll be coached for open water swimming by a professional triathlete and world record holder. As a third man, wearing a black shirt, purple shorts, and a pink cap, walks toward them, a social media overlay identifies him as 'Sam Laidlow,' an athlete and Kona bike course record holder, with the caption 'LET'S GET THIS BOOTY TO THE TOP.' The man in white introduces him, saying, "Please make some noise for Sam Laidlow!" to cheers and applause. Sam, with a distinct voice and slight accent, greets them warmly with a hug, joking, "Try to make you guys suffer!" The trio stands together by the water, continuing their conversation. The man in white refers to Sam as "My triathlete," and the man in black shirt shares his experience, stating, "I actually have talent for swimming, I think. I survived the water." Sam vividly describes the challenges of open water swimming, noting how it "feels like forever" and how one might think they're halfway only to find they've swum "400 meters." He also reveals his pre-swim ritual: "Normally I get on my knees before I swim and you baptize yourself."

The scene opens with a relaxed, joyful mood on a sunny beach, accompanied by gentle, uplifting instrumental music. The Man in white and green jersey, wearing an 'average' t-shirt, and Sam Laidlow, in a black t-shirt and pink cap, crouch by shallow, clear water, examining the sand. Sam gestures towards the water while the Man in white and green jersey holds a small blue object. They stand up, laughing and engaging in lighthearted conversation. Sam, speaking with a clear male voice, asks about Iron Man times, to which the Man in white and green jersey, also with a clear male voice, responds with "7:42, 7:43." He then recounts how he "broke the world record... by like eight or nine minutes, but it wasn't enough to win," mentioning a competitor named "Gustav." Sam exclaims "Motherfucker" in response. As they walk along a sandy path beside a dark stone wall, continuing their animated discussion, a third man, the Man in black shirt, joins them, laughing warmly. The Man in black shirt, speaking with a clear male voice, comments that "the water is very salty" and humorously calls the Man in white and green jersey "the ghetto version of Sam Laidlow." The Man in white and green jersey, smiling broadly, responds by calling himself "the little fat brother." The conversation continues with Sam asking if he has a "tri suit underneath or just trunks," to which the Man in white and green jersey replies "Naked," sparking laughter. The Man in white and green jersey then jokes about not bringing his trunks and swimming "naked" under his wetsuit, fearing he'll "get in prison." The Man in black shirt adds, "Let's all get naked now," prompting more laughter. The group stands together near the stone wall, with the Man in white and green jersey turning to face the camera and smiling before walking away, turning back to laugh as Sam and the Man in black shirt continue their conversation.

event_blocks.summary: A coach in a black wetsuit and yellow 'sailfish' swim cap, initially explaining his goal to observe swimming technique for improvement on a sandy beach, transitions to a coastal water area. There, he instructs a swimmer, also in a wetsuit, using a blue kickboard and emphasizing preparation for contact and chaos in an Iron Man triathlon simulation, humorously describing the need to 'bash the shit out of' the swimmer. The coach provides stroke correction, advising to 'get more water out of the same movement,' and engages in playful banter about peeing in the water. The swimmer begins to articulate the purpose of the 'swimming in an Iron Man' simulation. Later, three men in wetsuits are in shallow coastal water, discussing a 'lighter version' of an Iron Man swim. The swimmer, holding the blue kickboard, dives in, followed by the others, swimming and splashing near a stone breakwater. Upon returning to shore, they are greeted by a Man in a black shirt, who offers a towel and playful comments, leading to laughter and high-fives. The group then runs back into the water, laughing and splashing, with playful banter including 'my pants' and a disgusted 'No, that's disgusting man, what the fuck.' The coach warns that if they swim for more than an hour, he will stop helping. The segment concludes with swimmers in the water, accompanied by upbeat electronic music with female vocals, splashing and laughing, and a voice exclaiming the French phrase 'Le poulet c'est trop bon!'

Man in white and green jersey.description: Wearing a white t-shirt with 'average' printed on it, black shorts, and a watch on his left wrist; has curly dark hair and a mustache; seen smiling and interacting warmly on the beach path beside a stone wall. He is the primary instructor, speaking with a clear, instructional, and often humorous tone, explaining technical points and the triathlon simulation.

Beach setting.description: A sandy beach area adjacent to calm water, bordered by a stone wall, with palm trees and a building labeled 'Club Laganta' in the background; bright, sunny, and open, suggesting a coastal training or recreational location. The setting is accompanied by an upbeat, slightly comedic background music and the sounds of splashing during the water activities.

Swimmer in wetsuit.description: Wearing a black wetsuit and yellow 'sailfish' swim cap, goggles pushed up on forehead; holding a blue kickboard, actively swimming and coaching in shallow water, heard discussing the 'simulation' and 'lighter version' of the swim.

Coach in wetsuit.description: Wearing a black wetsuit and swim cap, adjusting gear and swimming alongside the other swimmer; appears to be participating in the simulation and contributing to the discussion about the 'lighter version' of the swim, later high-fiving the Man in black shirt.

Blue kickboard.description: Bright blue, rectangular kickboard held by the Swimmer in wetsuit during the swim simulation, used for support and training.

Man in black shirt.description: Wearing a black t-shirt and colorful patterned shorts, holding a towel; standing on the beach, laughing and interacting playfully with the swimmers, greeting them with a towel, receiving high-fives, and pointing and laughing as they re-enter the water.

raw_segments: The video opens with a Man in a white and green jersey, identified as the coach, standing on a sandy beach beside a stone wall under a bright blue sky. He speaks directly to the camera, explaining his goal to observe swimming technique for improvement, stating, "Nobody see anything. I just want to see you swim a little bit like this just so I can see if I can see any technical points that we can improve on." An upbeat, slightly comedic background music begins as the scene transitions to a coastal water area. Here, the coach, now in a black wetsuit and yellow 'sailfish' swim cap, stands in shallow water, gesturing animatedly. He instructs another swimmer, also in a black wetsuit and swim cap, who adjusts his gear before entering the calm bay. The coach continues to direct the swimmer, at one point holding a blue kickboard, while explaining the importance of preparing for contact and chaos in an Iron Man triathlon simulation. He humorously describes the need to "bash the shit out of" the swimmer to simulate race conditions, eliciting laughter. The coach provides detailed stroke correction, advising the swimmer to "get more water out of the same movement," to which the swimmer responds with "Okay, okay." They engage in playful banter about peeing in the water, with the coach noting "The lagoon's just got higher." The coach emphasizes using full strength for the stroke, and the swimmer, who is being coached, begins to articulate the purpose of the "swimming in an Iron Man" simulation before the segment ends. The overall atmosphere is instructional and energetic, set against a backdrop of a stone breakwater, palm trees, and distant hills, accompanied by splashing sounds during the water activity.

The video opens with three men in wetsuits standing in shallow coastal water under a bright, partly cloudy sky. The Swimmer in wetsuit, wearing a yellow 'sailfish' swim cap and holding a blue kickboard, gestures and speaks to the Coach in wetsuit and another swimmer, discussing a 'simulation' and 'a lighter version' of an Iron Man swim, with the Coach also contributing to the conversation. Upbeat electronic music begins as the Swimmer then dives into the water, using the blue kickboard for support, while the others follow, swimming and splashing through the calm water near a stone breakwater. Amidst the splashing and music, a voice exclaims, 'Got you, I got you,' and upon returning to shore, another voice confirms, 'They do that? Yeah, that's a lighter version.' They exit the water, shaking off water and adjusting their gear, where the Man in black shirt, wearing a black t-shirt and colorful patterned shorts, greets them with a towel and a playful comment, prompting laughter. A voice humorously remarks that 'sometimes they pull your leg back.' The Coach in wetsuit and the other swimmer high-five the Man in black shirt, as a voice declares, 'I think you guys are ready for it,' followed by an enthusiastic 'Alright, let's go.' The group then runs back into the water, laughing and splashing, while the Man in black shirt points and laughs at them. Amidst the renewed splashing and laughter, playful banter is heard, including 'my pants,' 'we start,' 'you sure,' 'I'm fine,' and a disgusted 'No, that's disgusting man, what the fuck.' As they continue in the water, a voice comments, 'That's quite nice actually,' followed by a humorous warning: 'If you guys do more than an hour, I'm giving up on helping you with your swimming.' The scene continues with the swimmers in the water, as a voice enthusiastically repeats the French phrase 'Le poulet c'est trop bon!' ('The chicken is too good!'), while the upbeat electronic music with female vocals continues, accompanying the swimmers as they splash and laugh, concluding the segment.

event_blocks.summary: The video begins with a title card for 'DAY 2 TRAINING 2 - 3000m SWIMMING' overlaid on a coastal view of the 'Club Laganta' building, accompanied by upbeat electronic music. Average Rob, wearing a wetsuit, executes freestyle strokes in calm, turquoise waters near a stone breakwater. During his swim, a male voice comments, "It's going well," followed by Average Rob's excited exclamations, "Oh, they come on, man, come on, man!" He then humorously anticipates "Aquaman" (Jason Momoa) emerging, declaring, "Not finished yet. I smoked him." Upon completing 3,831m, Average Rob wades ashore to be met by Sam Laidlow, who offers a towel and water bottle. Average Rob, raising an arm in celebration, states, "350 more and I have Iron Man distance." Sam Laidlow, with a distinct accent, remarks, "What a bitch," and inquires about his confidence and how the swim felt, to which Average Rob responds, "Amazing, yeah," amidst splashing sounds. Average Rob then confesses, "I did Iron Man distance. Also, I peed in my suit and then I was completely full," and Sam Laidlow adds, "You can't stop during Iron Man." The group, including another man in a white 'average' t-shirt, stands on the sunny, sandy beach, laughing. Sam Laidlow presents Average Rob with a blue energy bar, calling it "a little ice cream" and a present, eliciting an excited, "Ooh! It's the best ice cream I've ever seen!" Sam discusses training challenges, noting how "sometimes you lose the the envy to to fuck," with Average Rob agreeing, "Yeah."

You confident?", stating "You can't stop during Iron Man," offering "some presents, a little ice cream," and discussing training challenges, noting "sometimes you lose the the envy to to fuck."

He has a distinct, often excited or humorous voice, heard exclaiming "Oh, they come on, man, come on, man!", narrating his wait for "Aquaman" and playfully shouting "Jason Momoa!", stating "350 more and I have Iron Man distance," confirming "Amazing, yeah," humorously confessing "I did Iron Man distance. Also, I peed in my suit and then I was completely full," and excitedly reacting to ice cream with "Ooh! It's the best ice cream I've ever seen!" He also agrees with Sam by saying "Yeah."

event_blocks.summary: Riding through a vast, arid desert under a bright blue sky, a cyclist in a white Castelli jersey, white Canyon helmet, and orange-tinted sunglasses, speaking in a clear, slightly accented male voice, notes the "super windy" conditions making speed difficult but resolves to "keep my pace" and "not disappoint Nogle Frey," accompanied by the constant sound of wind and bicycle whirring. A second cyclist in a dark blue Castelli jersey, black helmet, and gold-tinted sunglasses joins him, and they ride in tandem, the second cyclist remarking on the "f***ing windy" yet "nice" conditions and urging to "look at the view." The first cyclist discusses avoiding a "gravel road" on their "time trial bike" to achieve their 60km goal. As they progress, the desert landscape transitions to a more developed area with stone walls, cacti, flowering bushes, and residential buildings. The first cyclist excitedly anticipates the 25km mark, exclaiming "25 more to go," and shouts "Let's go food! Yes!" in anticipation of dinner. Close-ups reveal their focused expressions. The first cyclist then encourages "standing up, let's go!" and later mentions "Rob is the guy today," as they continue through a narrow street lined with greenery and parked cars, the persistent wind and bike sounds remaining audible.

Subsequently, the Cyclist in green jersey, identifiable by a black helmet and gold-tinted sunglasses, addressed the camera, voicing his exasperation at being "lost like seven times already" due to GPS errors and feeling "stranded again," punctuated by an "Putain!" A low-angle shot focused on the front wheel of a blue bicycle revealed the rider's black cycling shoes and white socks with a 'SIXS' logo, pedaling on a paved road accompanied by the distinct sound of a bicycle bell, prompting a question about the noise. The perspective shifted to a rear view of the Cyclist in red jersey riding away on a straight road through a rural area with white houses and sparse vegetation, exclaiming "She reparation magique!" The Cyclist in red jersey then spoke to the camera while riding through a vast field of green crops under a bright blue sky with scattered clouds, describing the experience as a "roller coaster of emotions," admiring the "beautiful places," identifying potential wine grapes, and noting the "lot of wind" but concluding "the view is worth it," with the sound of strong wind audible. The Cyclist in red jersey's voice was heard stating the need to "hold my steering wheel with two hands or else I'm gonna die," calling it the "most windy ride of my life," before concluding "it was beautiful." The environments transitioned from desert to cultivated fields and then to an urban setting, all under clear skies with dynamic cloud formations, underscored by uplifting, rhythmic background music.

Triathletes don't quit." The Man in black shirt, Erno, joins him in a 'TOUT BIEN' t-shirt and patterned shorts, calling the quote "wisdom." The first man outlines the day's plan: "a 160k bike ride... and after 4-kilometer run," which Erno questions, "Immediately after?" The first man confirms it will be "quite fast also," suggests they "carb up and prepare for the day," and announces, "Let's go to the breakfast." The scene shifts to Erno eating oatmeal, pancakes, and banana at a dining table on the terrace, while the first man's voiceover states, "So we got some nice breakfast here. We're gonna need all the energy today," describing the 160k ride as "the longest I've ever ridden in my life" and predicting it will "feel like 360 kilometers today" due to "terrible" wind, accompanied by upbeat Latin-inspired music. The scene transitions to a bright, modern indoor corridor where the Man in white and green jersey, now in a triathlon suit, and Erno, in a green cycling jersey and helmet, prepare for their ride beside a blue bicycle. The first man greets the camera, "Hello, we're live," while Erno notes, "The wind is back." The first man then ponders, "So we're thinking about what gels we take on a 160-kilometer ride," as they examine a small device, indicating a structured training focus.

The Man in white and green jersey, now with blue-tinted sunglasses, explains that "yesterday was such a tough training" and they "didn't know the route," introducing a new rider named "Laurence." The Man in black shirt adds that Laurence is "a really good triathlete from Belgium." The Cyclist in green jersey confirms, "Today we're gonna do the Yaiza loop," which includes a "National Park" and "vineyards." The Man in white and green jersey details the elevation challenges: "Times three, 2,100 altitude meters," while the Man in black shirt adds that in Klagenfurt, they'll have to do "between 1,400, 1,500." The Cyclist in green jersey concludes it will be "the perfect training today." After a "Good luck, boys" from the Man in white and green jersey and a "Bye" from the Man in black shirt, the scene shifts to an outdoor setting with a paved road and volcanic terrain. Cycling sounds (pedaling and chain) accompany the group as they begin their ride. The Man in white and green jersey, now in a white Castelli jersey with orange and green accents and a white Canyon helmet, speaks to the camera, announcing they've completed "20K now" and are "riding through a gigantic volcano zone here," amidst rocky, barren hills under a partly cloudy sky.

event_blocks.summary: The Man in white and green jersey, sporting a white Canyon helmet and blue-tinted sunglasses, expressed excitement about a volcanic route, deeming it "100 times better than yesterday" and noting they were "almost done with loop one," even spotting camels. He rode with Lawrence, the Man in black shirt on a blue road bike, and the Cyclist in red jersey, shouting "Let's go!" as they completed the first loop and arrived at Club La Santa. After Lawrence's training was declared done, the Cyclist in red jersey mentioned a swim, and the Man in white and green jersey thanked them for guidance. Inside a corridor, he prepared his white road bike for "lap two," stating they had completed "55 kilometers" with "105 more to go," and planned to "catch" Rob in the descent. Later, navigating a paved road beside volcanic terrain, he recounted nearly falling twice downhill due to strong wind, forcing him to stop to eat. During lap two, he reported feeling good, with "gelikas" working well, and planned to refuel before going "full patat again." He then rode past white-walled buildings and palm trees, promising to return in "two more hours." Arriving at Club La Santa, he commented on the intense wind, and confirmed "Rob fell" while attempting a third round. The video concluded with him riding through a dry, rocky landscape and later standing beside his blue road bike, discussing the wind.

raw_segments: The video opens with a wide shot of a barren, volcanic landscape under a partly cloudy sky, where a group of cyclists is riding along a paved road, accompanied by upbeat music. The Man in white and green jersey, wearing a white Canyon helmet and blue-tinted sunglasses, speaks to the camera with excitement, exclaiming "Crazy!" and "What the fuck, there are camels!" He praises the route, stating it's "100 times better than yesterday," and announces they are "almost done with loop one." The scene shifts to show him riding alongside Lawrence, the Man in black shirt (wearing a green cycling jersey, black helmet, and pink socks on a blue road bike with 'DT SWISS' wheels), followed by the Cyclist in red jersey (wearing a black and red 'Velde man' jersey). As they ride through a scenic route with white-walled buildings and palm trees, the Man in white and green jersey shouts "Let's go!" and confirms "Loop one is done," as they arrive at a parking lot near the modern 'Club La Santa' building. He then addresses Lawrence, saying "Lawrence, your training is done for today," while Lawrence makes a brief comment, "Valasi, FC K." The Cyclist in red jersey adds, "Just a little swim afterwards," and the Man in white and green jersey thanks them for "showing us the ways." Inside a corridor with terracotta tiles, the Man in white and green jersey removes his helmet and sunglasses, revealing curly hair, and prepares his white road bike, mentioning he needs "to take some more gels." The Cyclist in red jersey humorously adds, "Need to put some extra Vaseline on the sack." The Man in white and green jersey then states, "It's not much to see, okay?" before announcing they've "reloaded" for "lap two," having completed "55 kilometers" with "105 more to go." He expresses his love for the route, noting "Rob disappeared" but he plans to "catch him in the descent," though he admits he'll "come closer." The video continues with him riding again through the rocky volcanic terrain, occasionally showing his handlebars and front wheel, and he recounts, "I just fell down," pointing out "a stream where I fell down."

The video opens with a close-up of a white road bike's front wheel and handlebars, showing a 'CONTINENTAL' tire and a black stem, as the rider navigates a paved road beside a rocky, volcanic terrain. A male voice, belonging to the rider, comments on nearly falling, saying, "like uh, two mullets on the ground. Look at this. Ay, ay, ay. But I'm fine. Well, I was lucky. I was very lucky. Imagine falling at this speed. Almost fell off the bike two times, in the downhill, and the wind just wanted to give me a butt hit, but I'll keep going. I have to literally stop to eat because there's too much wind." The camera then shifts to a first-person perspective of the Man in white and green jersey, wearing a white Canyon helmet and blue-tinted sunglasses, speaking to the camera while riding through a scenic, arid landscape under a partly cloudy sky, with background music playing. He asks, "Hello, hello. How's it going? Lap two went well. You don't look tired at all. No, I'm really good. The gelikas are really good. What the hell? So now we need to refuel one last time and then go full patat again." He transitions to wearing gold-tinted sunglasses, continuing to speak as he rides past white-walled buildings and palm trees, stating, "I'll be back in exactly two hours. Ciao, ciao. Two more hours, come on." The scene cuts to a third-person view of the same cyclist riding through a parking lot, passing parked cars and a white van, before turning onto a road. He then dismounts and walks his bike toward a modern building labeled 'Club La Santa', where he waves to the camera, while commenting on the intense wind, "There is so much more wind. Yo, I'm 1993, something like that. The wind is just fucked up. I don't even know if you can hear me." Another voice interjects, "Rob fell," which the rider confirms, "Rob fell. Yeah, he fell. He was going for a third round." The video returns to a first-person view of the Man in white and green jersey, now wearing a black helmet and gold-tinted sunglasses, riding through a dry, rocky landscape with distant hills. The final shot shows him standing beside his blue road bike, wearing a green cycling jersey with orange accents, black helmet, and gold-tinted sunglasses, gesturing with his hands as he speaks, with a modern building and palm trees in the background.

event_blocks.summary: The Man in white and green jersey, wearing a white Canyon helmet and blue-tinted sunglasses, prepared for a ride by retrieving a water bottle from a white locker in a modern corridor amidst muffled chatter and well wishes. He then transitioned to his white road bike outdoors under a bright blue sky, embarking on a challenging route through scenic coastal areas and barren volcanic terrain. Early in the ride, he encountered mechanical issues with his derailleur, characterized by loud clicking, grinding, and clanking sounds, losing his three lowest gears. Despite this, he continued, expressing frustration with the "fucking wind" in Lanzarote, describing his ride as "time trial modus." Later, he reflected on a "horrible beautiful thing" he had done, mentioning the wind being "10 times worse" on the third loop and that it was his longest ride at 165.05 km with 2,117 m elevation gain. Transitioning to a 4km run, he dismounted his bike near a blue running track, grunting with exertion. He ran on the track, with palm trees and white buildings in the background, audibly breathing heavily and expressing pain in French, "Aiyayay. Ça fait mal." A Strava overlay confirmed a 4.20 km run at a pace of 5:17/km, which he concluded with a smile and a thumbs-up to the camera.

raw_segments: The video opens with the Man in white and green jersey, wearing a white Canyon helmet and blue-tinted sunglasses, retrieving a water bottle from a white locker in a modern corridor, accompanied by muffled chatter and a male voice commenting, "It's weird when you listen," followed by laughter and an encouraging "run, run, run, run." Another voice wishes "Good luck," to which the Man in white and green jersey replies "Thank you" after being told "Don't fall," promising "I'll try." He then transitions to riding his white road bike outdoors under a bright blue sky, stating, "50 more kilometers. Last lap. Come on," and describing the "First part is always the hardest. 7 kilometers going up." As he rides through a scenic coastal area and into barren volcanic terrain, he lets out a grunt of exertion, exclaiming "Fuck," and reveals, "The climbing starts and there's something wrong with my derailleur." Loud mechanical clicking, grinding, and clanking sounds are heard, which he points out, saying, "You hear this sound? This is not normal for a bike." The camera cuts to the Man in black shirt, wearing a green cycling jersey and black helmet, pedaling vigorously on his blue road bike, with a Garmin device displaying metrics like heart rate (171) and distance (144.82 km), while the Man in white and green jersey continues to diagnose his bike issue, stating, "Okay, I lost my two lowest [gears], try third," then correcting himself, "Okay, no, I lost my three lowest." He reappears, speaking directly to the camera with a wide, sweaty smile, exclaiming, "Only 20 more kilometers to go and the wind, it's fucking annoying!" He adds, "I'm riding 13.8 in time trial modus, it's just... worst thing about Lanzarote is the fucking wind," concluding with "Look." The scene dynamically shifts from indoor preparation to intense outdoor cycling, emphasizing physical exertion, scenic environment, and the challenges faced.

The video opens with a first-person perspective of a cyclist riding a white road bike along a straight, empty road through a barren, volcanic landscape under a bright blue sky. The Man in white and green jersey, wearing a white Canyon helmet and blue-tinted sunglasses, comments with an accented voice, "Almost flat. Going so fucking slow." Cinematic music begins as the scene cuts to a low-angle shot of the rocky, gravelly terrain. A close-up then shows the Man speaking directly to the camera with an energetic expression, reflecting, "That was the most horrible beautiful thing I've ever done in my life. The wind, third loop was 10 times worse than the second," adding, "I know he's gonna cry." He continues riding his white road bike through a parking lot and a corridor with white walls and palm trees, as a Strava activity overlay appears, detailing 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 119'—a 165.05 km ride with 2,117 m elevation gain, noted as his longest. He announces, "Straight into 4K run," and exclaims, "Fuck, longest day of my life!" He then plans, "I think I need to run 5:30 the kilometer. 40 more minutes." The music continues as he dismounts, the sound of bike gears audible, and walks his bike toward a blue running track. Removing his helmet, he grunts, "Oh my god," and transitions into a 4km run, as indicated by a text overlay. He expresses disbelief, "Can't believe it. For lowest start from here." The camera captures him running on the track, with palm trees and white buildings in the background, his heavy breathing and running sounds audible. He exclaims in French, "Aiyayay. Ça fait mal," indicating pain. A low-angle shot shows him adjusting his bike, now wearing a green cycling jersey, before he resumes running. The final frames show him running on the track, a Strava overlay confirming a 4.20 km run at a pace of 5:17/km, concluding with a smile and a thumbs-up to the camera.

event_blocks.summary: The Man in white and green jersey, exhausted after a run confirmed by a Strava overlay showing 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 119' and 4.20 km at a 5:17/km pace, expressed concern about another individual's performance in windy conditions. raw_segments: The video opens with the Man in white and green jersey standing on a blue running track, visibly exhausted and breathing heavily, sighing, "What a heavy day." A Strava overlay confirms 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 119' with a 4.20 km run at a 5:17/km pace. He adjusts his sunglasses, drinks from a shaker bottle, and speaks to the camera, expressing concern: "I hope he's fine because if wind was his worst thing, then last loop for him is probably going to be the toughest thing he's ever done in his life." The scene transitions to a man in a green cycling jersey, black helmet, and gold-tinted sunglasses, riding a blue road bike through a volcanic landscape. He speaks to the camera, explaining, "Hola! I fucking fixed it. So I took my wheel out completely... And it works again," accompanied by sounds of bike adjustments and clicking. He continues, sharing his achievement: "I broke my record. I went above 90. Same wind as loop two. I have to punch as a motherfucker." Close-ups show his face and the rugged terrain. He then arrives at a parking lot near a white building, dismounts his bike, and is greeted by the Man in white and green jersey, who excitedly shouts, "Come on, Arlo! Let's go, Arlo! Let's go! Yes! What the fuck!" as upbeat, celebratory music begins. The Man in white and green jersey gives the cyclist a high-five. The cyclist, still excited, confirms, "Bro, I beat my record!" and when asked "How much?", replies, "I went above 90." The greeter exclaims "What?" and the cyclist adds, "Look. Newest. 92.6." The greeter repeats, "92.6!" and the cyclist concludes, "I went crazy." A Strava overlay appears, showing 'Morning Ride' with 165.31 km and 2,003 m elevation gain, labeled as 'arno's longest ride on Strava!' The two men interact, with the cyclist adjusting his bike and gear, while the Man in white and green jersey checks his Garmin device, which displays metrics like 144.82 km distance and 171 heart rate. The environment is a structured parking lot with parked cars and white walls under consistent daylight, with the celebratory music continuing.

event_blocks.summary: The video opens in a sunny parking lot where Marco, in a white Castelli jersey and blue-tinted sunglasses, greets Arno, who arrives on a blue road bike with 'DT SWISS' wheels, wearing a green cycling jersey, black helmet, and pink socks. Arno adjusts his bike, checks his Garmin, and recounts a past issue, stating, "was a problem, I fixed it. I went angry," to which Marco responds, "You went uh..." Arno continues, "Third lap I didn't..." with a distinct bike gear shifting sound, then exclaims, "I didn't realize... Holy shit balls, Marco," revealing Marco's name, before adding, "Bro, I went Super Saiyan on the last lap." The scene shifts to a bright rooftop terrace with white buildings and palm trees, where Spanish-style background music plays. Marco comments, "Little hacker," and asks about Arno's watch, which Arno confirms is in the apartment. Marco then states, "I told him," and Arno playfully retorts, "Yeah, I'm not your slave, eh," and asks, "Who forgets his watch?" The scene transitions to Arno running on a bright blue synthetic track, surrounded by greenery and white buildings, as the Spanish music intensifies with a male vocalist. A Strava overlay appears for Arno's "Avondloop" activity: 4.09 km distance, 6:21/km pace, 26m 2s time, identifying him as "arno The Kid." Marco praises, "Nice shot." After finishing, Arno walks back to Marco, who is sitting on a low wall. Arno remarks, "I'm actually not that tired. I thought I was going to be..." before Marco interjects, "So proud of us." They high-five and laugh. Arno humorously adds, "I have to take a shit, bro. What the fuck?" Marco replies, "Really proud." Arno removes his jersey, revealing a black heart rate monitor, and Marco praises, "You did it. It was amazing. You did it too." Sitting together and drinking water, Arno reflects, "160k, 4k run. We did it and still feeling good actually," and outlines his plans: "Protein shake, little shower, 'cause I need it. And tomorrow we do it again." The Spanish music fades to a melancholic guitar melody in the sunny, coastal training environment.
