His voice, with a distinct Belgian/Dutch accent, details his plan: "So today we need to do 3K of swimming and then 80K bike riding," adding "It's going nice, I gotta go. Bye bye." He continues swimming, using a black swim paddle, explaining his remaining distance and technique: "300 meters left, just with the bare hands and I have 800 with pedals and then cooling down." He notes the 50-meter pool "feels a bit like open water because it's also super deep," and outlines the final sets: "4 times 200 and then 200 cooling down and then we're done. Let's go!" After finishing, he rests at the pool's edge, removing his cap and goggles, checking his smartwatch, and speaking to the camera, sounding relaxed and satisfied: "Alright, we're done. 3.4K. Feeling nice, feeling good. It's such a nice place here." On-screen text confirms his Strava activity as 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 120'. He then plans the next steps, stating, "Then we're gonna get some lunch and then we're gonna go for a bike ride." The scene transitions to an outdoor dining area at 'EL LAGO' under large white umbrellas, where Rob, now in a maroon t-shirt, sits with a companion in a black shirt, enjoying a colorful meal and red juice. The companion, a man in a black shirt, exclaims "Oh, wow!" and "Man, wow!" and later says "Gracias." Rob's voiceover explains they are "Loading up the carbs," but expresses frustration that "Our planning today was is a bit shit. We still have 80K to ride. It's 3:30."

The video opens at El Lago, where Average Rob, sporting a mustache and a maroon t-shirt, engages with the camera while eating a colorful dish under large white umbrellas. Across from him, the Man in black shirt, in a black t-shirt, focuses on his meal, using a fork and knife, with a blue water bottle and phone beside him. The Man in black shirt's voice immediately sets a humorous tone, explaining their cycling dilemma: they need to wait an hour for food to digest, then it will be 5 PM before they leave, followed by a 3 to 3.5-hour ride, making it too late for food. He suggests faking an injury 'with Frea' or doing a shorter 60km ride. The scene transitions to an indoor apartment. The Man in black shirt, now shirtless in black underwear, gestures in a bathroom, his voice continuing to express reluctance, stating, 'I don't wanna train,' and proposing a 55km loop instead of 80km, as 80km would take three and a half hours in Lanzarote. Average Rob, now in a red and white cycling jersey, white helmet, and red-tinted sunglasses, walks through the apartment, adjusting his gear, pausing to look at a photo taped to the wall, and filling a water bottle in the kitchen. Average Rob's voice interjects enthusiastically, 'So I can break my record,' and 'My 92.56 kilometers an hour!' The Man in black shirt responds, 'No, don't do it,' and later, 'He's gonna be extremely disappointed. Frea for today,' followed by laughter. The Man in black shirt then appears in a black cycling jersey and helmet, adjusting gear near a blue 'DT SWISS' bicycle. His voice continues, 'Don't look. I'm not a big fan of training. I've been complaining the whole day.' Average Rob checks his Garmin device. Average Rob's voice asks, 'Really?' to which the Man in black shirt replies, 'Welcome to the real world now,' with more laughter, concluding with 'It's time for you...' The segment captures a shift from a relaxed meal to the humorous and slightly reluctant preparation for a challenging bike ride.

The minute opens inside a modern apartment where the Man in black shirt, now in a black cycling jersey, helmet, and gold-tinted sunglasses, prepares his blue bicycle. Average Rob, in a red and white cycling jersey, white helmet, and red-tinted sunglasses, joins him, and they exchange a high-five. The Man in black shirt delivers a humorous, motivational monologue, first in Spanish ("Take your cojones, vamos!") then in English, stating, "Sometimes in life, everything is against you, but God is never against you," followed by laughter. The scene then transitions to an outdoor volcanic landscape under a bright blue sky, as an upbeat instrumental track begins, accompanied by the sound of wind. On-screen text reads 'DAY 4 TRAINING 7 55KM - BIKE RIDE'. As they begin their 55km bike ride through the arid, rocky terrain with sparse vegetation and distant hills, the Man in black shirt, his curly hair blowing in the wind, speaks to the camera, detailing their morning swim and the 55km bike ride. He recounts Average Rob's suggestion to "take it easy today" and "ride together," before humorously noting that Rob, whom he refers to as "Arno," "disappears" on the first 30-minute climb, where he "always drops Arno," but expects Arno to catch him on the descent. Average Rob then addresses the camera, smiling and breathing heavily, emphasizing the strong wind blowing through the volcanic zone and pointing out a lone palm tree in the distance.

The video opens with close-up shots of two cyclists, Average Rob and the Man in black shirt, both wearing helmets and sunglasses, speaking to the camera against a backdrop of a bright blue sky with scattered clouds and a volcanic landscape. Average Rob, in a red jersey and white helmet with red-tinted sunglasses, introduces the segment in a clear voice, stating, "Alright, so. We're on our way up because I want to show you guys Arno's 92 km/h descent." He also affectionately refers to the Man in black shirt as "Arno," saying, "Voilà, that's Arno. He does love me, he waited for me," and later asks, "Arno, you ready?" The Man in black shirt, in a black jersey and black helmet with gold-tinted sunglasses, smiles and gestures excitedly. The scene transitions to wide aerial views of a long, winding road cutting through a barren, rocky volcanic terrain. The Man in black shirt, Arno, stands beside his blue bicycle with 'DT SWISS' on its wheel, pointing out the descent route and describing its challenging, bumpy nature. He explains, "The descent is here. On camera it doesn't look crazy, but here... There's like platforms going down, and that's where... you see the bump, where the car is? It's all bumps, so it's like... Turbo, turbo, turbo." He emphasizes the strong, audible wind, stating, "As you can hear, there's a lot of wind." The camera then captures Arno riding down the steep road at high speed, with the camera following from behind and then from a side angle, showcasing the dramatic descent through the rugged landscape. An upbeat, driving instrumental track begins as he descends. The road is flanked by dark volcanic rock and sparse vegetation, with distant hills and a few scattered buildings visible under the partly cloudy sky. On-screen text overlays provide context, including phrases like 'Arno's 92 km/h descent' and 'It's all bumps, so it's like... Turbo, turbo, turbo,' highlighting the intensity of the ride. The segment concludes with Arno continuing his descent, the wind whipping around him and the sounds of his bike on the bumpy road, as he navigates the steep, winding road.

event_blocks.summary: Average Rob, clad in a red jersey, cycles his white road bike through a barren, winding landscape, pausing at a roundabout to check his Strava activity overlay, which displays 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 118' and 55.19 km. Arno The Kid joins him, and after a brief exchange about hydration, Average Rob expresses satisfaction with his ride, noting his energy levels and leg strength, before declaring "Day four is a wrap" and mentioning the need to "reload some proteins." The narrative shifts to 'DAY 5 last training day' and an 'IRONMAN SIMULATION 3000 m' as a man in a white t-shirt with 'Stop Working Start Drinking' on the back walks a blue bicycle toward a coastal area. This man, identified as Milan (Man in white t-shirt with mustache), explains the 'IRONMAN SIMULATION' involves a "swimming 3K, then straight into a 40K bike ride, and then straight into a 16K run," emphasizing "smooth transitions" for a "full Ironman." He is joined by Arno (Man in white t-shirt with curly hair), and after retrieving their bikes from a glass-enclosed storage area, they prepare at a concrete seating area labeled 'Club La Santa'. Milan reveals a custom "Castelli" triathlon suit, which Arno identifies as a "Borat suit" due to its "full weird" pink and green design. At the Club La Santa outdoor swimming pool, Milan and Arno, wearing matching triathlon suits, swim caps, and goggles, begin the simulation after a "Three, two, one, go!" countdown, with Milan diving in first. Milan emerges from the pool an hour later, his Strava showing a 3,050m swim in 50m 59s, and he transitions to a blue 'DT SWISS' bicycle for the 40km bike ride through a dry, rocky desert landscape, reflecting on his swim performance. Arno is briefly shown exiting the water after a 2,950m 'Lunch Swim'.

Strava activity overlay.description: On-screen graphic displaying workout details including 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 121' with 3,050m distance, 50m 59s time, and 1:40/100m pace, timestamped May 18, 2023 at 12:10 PM; later shows 'Lunch Swim' for Arno The Kid with 2,950m distance, 59m 10s time, and 2:00/100m pace.

Blue bicycle.description: A blue road bicycle with 'DT SWISS' written on the wheel, ridden by the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) through a dry, rocky desert landscape under a clear blue sky.

On-screen text overlay.description: White text on a black background reading '1 HOUR LATER' and later 'TRIATHLON PT. 2 40KM - BIKE RIDE', indicating time progression and the next phase of the triathlon simulation.

Arno The Kid.description: Wearing a black cycling jersey, black helmet, and gold-tinted sunglasses; exits the pool after a swim, with his Strava data overlay showing 'Lunch Swim' for 2,950m in 59m 10s at 2:00/100m; sits on a concrete bench under a covered area, drying off with an orange towel. He speaks with a distinct voice, reflecting on his swim ('Done. It's not amazing.') and asking the Man in white t-shirt about his run ('How was your run?').

Man in white t-shirt.description: Wearing a white t-shirt with 'Stop Working Start Drinking' printed on the back, blue shorts, and carrying a black bag; walking along a paved path beside a pool and white buildings, pushing a blue bicycle, under a bright blue sky with palm trees. He is the primary speaker, explaining the Ironman simulation details, the custom 'Castelli' triathlon suit, and its 'weird' movie-inspired design, speaking in a clear, explanatory, and later amused tone.

Average Rob.description: Wearing a red cycling jersey with white horizontal stripes, a white helmet, red-tinted sunglasses, and blue cycling shorts; standing beside his white road bike, checking his phone, and interacting with Arno The Kid in a volcanic landscape under a partly cloudy sky. His voice is heard greeting Arno, stating his 55 km distance, humorously noting 'If it's not on Strava, it never happened,' offering water, discussing his energy levels ('I'm not tired, it's crazy,' 'my legs were feeling super good'), expressing enjoyment for the ride ('I really like this ride,' 'might be one of my favorite ones'), planning post-ride recovery ('get some food again, reload some proteins'), making playful grunting sounds, and setting up the 'Day five' 'Ironman simulation.'

White road bike.description: A white road bike with black handlebars and a black seat, ridden by Average Rob through the volcanic terrain and later parked beside him during the interaction with Arno The Kid.

Club La Santa.description: A modern building with a sign reading 'Club La Santa', located near a parking lot with palm trees and white-walled buildings, serving as a stop for the cyclists; visible as the Man in white t-shirt approaches and waves to the camera.

Concrete seating area.description: A tiered, open-air concrete seating structure under a covered roof at Club La Santa, with visible backpacks and gear on the steps, serving as the preparation zone for the simulation.

Triathlon suit.description: A pink and green full-body triathlon suit with a white front panel featuring a pattern of small black dots, green vertical stripes on the sides, and red circular logos on the shoulders; worn by the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) during the simulation preparation. It is a custom 'Castelli' brand suit, described as 'full weird' and based on a favorite movie, specifically identified as a 'Borat suit'.

Man in white t-shirt (curly hair).description: Wearing the same pink and green triathlon suit; has curly hair and a visible tattoo on his left arm; identified by Milan as 'Arno' (0:35); checks his watch, gestures while talking, states 'The deeper, the better' (0:30), suggests 'Okay, let's get started, boys' (0:32), confirms 'Yeah' (0:39) regarding the course on his Garmin, and later wears a swim cap and goggles as he prepares to swim; stands by the poolside before entering the water.

Outdoor swimming pool.description: A large, modern outdoor pool with multiple lanes, blue water, white starting blocks numbered 8 and 9, metal handrails, and decorative blue-and-white bunting; surrounded by white buildings and palm trees under a sunny sky. Sounds of splashing and muffled underwater movements are heard when swimmers are in the water.

Man in white t-shirt (mustache).description: Wearing a pink and green triathlon suit with a white front panel featuring small black dots, green vertical stripes on the sides, and red circular logos on the shoulders; has a mustache and a visible tattoo on his left forearm; transitions from swimming to cycling, putting on a white helmet and red-tinted sunglasses, and riding a blue bicycle through a desert landscape. He speaks frequently with a clear, articulate voice, encouraging himself ('Come on bro, let's go!'), explaining his actions ('Gonna dry up because I don't want to get cold. There's a lot of wind.'), reflecting on his swim ('Swimming went well... happy with the swim.'), and conversing with Arno The Kid ('Good. And you're not too far behind.').

raw_segments: The segment opens with a first-person perspective of a hand pointing toward a desolate volcanic landscape beside a paved road under a bright blue sky, followed by a view from a moving vehicle showing cacti and distant hills. Upbeat, rhythmic string music begins as Average Rob, in a red jersey, cycles his white road bike along a winding road through the barren terrain. He stops at a roundabout, checking his phone, which displays a Strava activity overlay for 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 118' showing 55.19 km. Average Rob greets "Hello" to Arno The Kid, who joins him, also checking his Strava data. Average Rob's voice is heard stating "55 kilometers" and humorously adding, "If it's not on Strava, it never happened." The two cyclists interact, sharing water, with Average Rob offering, "You want Arno?" to which Arno replies, "I didn't drink enough." Average Rob, gesturing and smiling, discusses his energy, remarking, "I'm not tired, it's crazy. My legs were feeling super good. Once the motor was warm, I was feeling like super good. I really like this ride. Might be one of my favorite ones," while Arno questions, "Really?" Average Rob then declares, "Day four is a wrap. Now we need to get some food again, reload some proteins, to get these muscles," making playful grunting sounds and adding, "Look at these jambons." The string music returns as the scene shifts to a bright, sunny day with an on-screen title card reading 'DAY 5 last training day' and 'IRONMAN SIMULATION 3000 m'. Average Rob's voice continues, setting the stage for "day five, final day also of our training camp here. We're going to do a simulation today, an Ironman simulation," as a man in a white t-shirt with 'Stop Working Start Drinking' on the back walks along a palm-lined path with a blue bicycle toward a coastal area with white buildings and a pool.

The video opens with a Man in a white t-shirt (mustache) walking along a sunlit, palm-lined path beside a modern white building, pushing a blue bicycle. On-screen text announces an 'IRONMAN SIMULATION' with segments '3000 m', '40 KM', and '16 KM' appearing sequentially, which the Man with mustache's voice explains as "swimming 3K, then straight into a 40K bike ride, and then straight into a 16K run." He adds that they're setting everything aside for "smooth transitions" in this "full Ironman" simulation, clarifying it's "like a quarter triathlon but with more swimming and more running." He is soon joined by another man, the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair), wearing yellow-tinted sunglasses and a 'haha.' t-shirt. They walk together, conversing, and approach a glass-enclosed storage area where they retrieve their bikes, accompanied by a clanking sound. The Man with mustache remarks, "The bikes are already here. Feels like an Ironman already." The scene shifts to a concrete seating area under a covered structure labeled 'Club La Santa', where they sit and prepare. The Man with mustache states, "I knew there was going to be a race day today, so I prepared something special," referring to a custom "Castelli" triathlon suit. The Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) removes his shirt, revealing a tattoo, while the Man with mustache unpacks a pink and green triathlon suit from a bag, explaining it's "full weird" and "based off of one of my favorite movies of all time." The Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) interjects, "I think I know what it is," before excitedly exclaiming, "It's a Borat suit!" as the Man with mustache laughs and confirms, "On a triathlon suit." Upbeat, slightly comedic music begins as the Man with mustache puts on the suit, zipping it up, and stands up, smiling, ready for the simulation. The Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) reacts with an enthusiastic "Yes, yes!" and asks, "Castelli, yeah?" The background shows other participants and a bright, open coastal environment, suggesting a warm, sunny day at a training facility.

The video opens at Club La Santa, showing two men in matching pink and green triathlon suits at a concrete seating area under a covered structure. The Man in white t-shirt (mustache), identified as Milan, adjusts his suit while holding a plastic-wrapped item. He addresses the camera directly, speaking in a clear voice about a friend named Stykel who makes cycling outfits, and how he secured a "one of a kind" suit for a giveaway. Milan instructs viewers to "subscribe, like the video, and then comment your favorite motivational quote" to be entered to win this "collector's item," aligning with the on-screen text prompts 'SUBSCRIBE', 'LIKE THE VIDEO', and 'COMMENT MOTIVATION'. His companion, the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair), identified as Arno, checks his watch and gestures animatedly. As they prepare to move, Arno states, "The deeper, the better," which Milan echoes, before Arno suggests, "Okay, let's get started, boys." Milan then wishes Arno "Good luck" and asks if he has the course on his Garmin, to which Arno confirms "Yeah." Milan then says, "Milan, you say 'sterkte'," before declaring, "Wait, I go in the water." The scene transitions to a bright, sunny outdoor swimming pool where Milan and Arno, now in swim caps and goggles, stand by the starting blocks. After Milan gives Arno a high-five, a countdown of "Three, two, one, go!" leads to Milan diving into the clear blue water with a splash. The camera follows him underwater as he swims freestyle, showcasing his technique, accompanied by upbeat Latin music. Text overlays announce 'IRONMAN SIMULATION' with distances 3000m, 40KM, 16KM, and later 'TRIATHLON PT. 1 3000m - SWIMMING', emphasizing the structured nature of the training.

The segment opens with upbeat Latin/Reggaeton music featuring a female vocalist as the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) swims freestyle in a bright, modern outdoor pool, accompanied by splashing and muffled underwater sounds. After an on-screen text '1 HOUR LATER', he emerges, smiling in a pink and green triathlon suit, pulling himself up a metal ladder. His Strava overlay reveals 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 121' with a 3,050m swim completed in 50m 59s. He encourages himself with a clear, articulate voice, saying, "Yeah, come on bro, come on. Alrighty. Let's go. Time to waste," before wrapping himself in an orange towel. He explains, "Gonna dry up because I don't want to get cold. There's a lot of wind," as he walks briskly to a covered area. Preparing for the next leg, he puts on a white cycling helmet and red-tinted sunglasses, then mounts a blue 'DT SWISS' bicycle. As he rides through a dry, rocky desert landscape under a clear blue sky, a close-up shows him speaking, with 'TRIATHLON PT. 2 40KM - BIKE RIDE' on screen. He reflects on his swim, stating, "Alright, just had to switch. Went quite slow, I'd say, but I'd prefer it to be slow and good and to be in a hurry and forget shit. Swimming went well. In the beginning, my arms were a bit heavy, but once they were warmed up, it actually went quite well, so happy with the swim." The scene briefly cuts back to the pool where Arno The Kid exits the water, his Strava showing a 2,950m 'Lunch Swim'. Arno remarks in a distinct voice, "Done. It's not amazing," as he sits on a concrete bench, drying off with an orange towel. The Man in white t-shirt continues his ride in the desert, and a brief exchange is heard where Arno asks, "How was your run?" and the Man in white t-shirt replies, "Good. And you're not too far behind," as the upbeat music continues, emphasizing the transition from the structured poolside to the open, arid terrain.

event_blocks.summary: At Club La Santa, a man in a pink and green triathlon suit, identified as Arno, prepares for a run, reflecting on past training and feeling confident. He mounts his blue bicycle and rides through a desert landscape, noting the challenging, sandy terrain and expressing determination for the remaining 20 kilometers. He pauses to eat a banana, calling it the "fourth discipline of a triathlon" and his "favorite thing to do in the world," shouting "Carbs! Carbs!" before anticipating his arrival in Milan. Later, Arno, on a white road bike, rides through a volcanic landscape, commenting on his "extra mash" and transitioning from cycling to running after checking his Garmin device, which shows a 144.82 km distance and 43:55 time. He plans four loops of 4 kilometers for a 16K run, feeling "fresh" and "good." The scene shifts to a first-person perspective of running on a paved path in a bright, sunny desert with sparse vegetation and distant mountains. He high-fives another man, also in a white t-shirt, who asks about his well-being. Arno explains initial stiffness from the bike and swim, expresses pride in "Arno" for the 16K, and humorously discusses needing to manage hydration and potential flatulence. He accepts water from his companion, gulping it down and commenting on its warmth, before continuing his run with a determined expression against a coastal backdrop featuring the ocean and parking areas.

Desert running path.description: A gravel path in a dry, rocky desert landscape under a clear blue sky, where the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) begins his ride through a volcanic zone, with sand dunes, sparse vegetation, and distant mountains visible, accompanied by the sounds of wind and the rider's commentary on 'sand spots' and 'riding through the desert'.

Blue bicycle.description: A blue road bicycle with 'DT SWISS' written on the wheel, ridden by the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) through a roundabout. Sounds of a bicycle chain shifting and clicking are heard during the cycling segments.

Man in white t-shirt (mustache).description: Wearing a pink and green triathlon suit with a white front panel featuring small black dots, green vertical stripes on the sides, and red circular logos on the shoulders; has a mustache and curly hair; running along a paved path beside a road, speaking to the camera with a clear, slightly accented voice, expressing satisfaction, humor, and dehydration; gesturing with his hands, occasionally checking his watch, and sharing water.

Concrete seating area.description: A tiered, open-air concrete seating structure under a covered roof at Club La Santa, with visible backpacks and gear on the steps, serving as the preparation zone for the simulation, where the man engages in conversation and receives farewells before his ride.

Strava activity overlay.description: On-screen graphic displaying workout details, including 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 121' and later 'Afternoon Ride' for 'Arno The Kid,' which aligns with the main subject being referred to as 'Arno' in the audio.

Garmin device.description: A black Garmin cycling computer mounted on the handlebars, displaying metrics such as timer (43:55), distance (144.82 km), heart rate (171), speed (12.6 km/h), cadence (64), power (131), and time of day (5:09); later seen being checked by the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) during the transition.

Club La Santa.description: A modern building with a sign reading 'Club La Santa', located near a parking lot with palm trees and white-walled buildings, serving as a stop for the cyclists; visible as the Man in white t-shirt approaches and transitions between activities.

Parking lot.description: An asphalt parking area with white lines, parked cars, and a white building in the background, serving as the endpoint of the ride and the location of the transition between cycling and running.

Volcanic terrain.description: A barren, rocky landscape with dark soil, sparse vegetation, and distant hills under a partly cloudy sky, serving as the backdrop for the bike ride and descent.

White road bike.description: A white road bike with black handlebars and a black seat, ridden by the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) through the volcanic terrain. Sounds of a bicycle chain shifting and clicking are heard during the cycling segments.

Man in white t-shirt (curly hair).description: Wearing the same pink and green triathlon suit; has curly hair and a visible tattoo on his left arm; running alongside the Man in white t-shirt (mustache), interacting with a distinct voice, asking questions, offering water, sharing a high-five, and displaying an energetic and joyful expression.

raw_segments: The video opens with the Man in white t-shirt (mustache), wearing a pink and green triathlon suit, sitting on a concrete bench at Club La Santa, adjusting his gear. He reflects in a male voice that someone was "really good" and "helped my technique," making him feel like "a kid next to him." A bell rings, followed by farewells like "Bye-bye," "Good luck," and "Ciao" from multiple voices. He then puts on a black cycling helmet, stands, mounts his blue bicycle, and begins riding along a paved road through a desert landscape. The sound of wind picks up as he states, "Just riding through the desert." The camera switches to a first-person perspective, showing the road, sky, and his helmet. He exclaims "Oh shit," and "Oh my god," noting "there's like these sand spots, it's so sketchy, holy shit," indicating a challenging terrain. He then reflects on his performance, mentioning the "first 10k" where he "went really fast" and "a lot of aero," even with "a lot of wind." With the wind still audible, he declares, "20 more to go, let's go," and then, "Let's go, a little climb now." He expresses satisfaction, stating "all these months of training are paying off," and that his "legs feel really good," allowing him to "go deep and then recover really fast." The wind continues to blow as he says, "Let's go!" He stops to eat a banana, holding it up to the camera before taking a bite, explaining that "the fourth discipline of a triathlon is eating," and it's his "favorite thing to do in the world." He emphatically calls out "Carbs! Carbs!" before concluding, "One more kilometer and we're there. Can't wait to see Milan. He's gonna be so proud, I can feel it." The bright, sunny desert environment with sand dunes, sparse vegetation, and distant mountains remains visible throughout his determined ride.

The segment opens with the Man in a pink and green triathlon suit, identified as Arno, riding a white road bike through a dry, rocky volcanic landscape under a partly cloudy sky, accompanied by upbeat electronic music. A voice exclaims, "And 16K run, let's go!" As Arno passes parked cars and approaches the 'Club La Santa' building, he comments, "The extra mash, I like," and humorously adds, "My name is Borat. The extra mash." He continues, "I like this, I just did 40 kilometers bicycle ride," before stating, "Let's go for the switch up," as bicycle chain shifting sounds are heard. He dismounts, removes his helmet, and enters the modern building. Inside, he changes into orange running shoes, preparing for the next leg. Another voice asks, "How was Arno's swim? Was he happy?" to which Arno, breathing heavily, replies, "Yeah, he was happy, he was fresh," and later, "Feeling fresh." The voice remarks, "You look full triathlete now," and Arno confidently responds, "Yeah, now I look like a pro." After more bicycle chain sounds, he exits the building, jogs through the parking lot, and transitions to running on a gravel path through the desert. The scene briefly shows him riding a blue bicycle through a roundabout. Back at the building, Arno sits, checking his Garmin device which displays metrics like 144.82 km distance and 43:55 time. He explains his plan: "Four loops of 4 kilometers, 16K, and then we're done for today." The other voice echoes, "The extra mash," and Arno enthusiastically declares, "I like running, you like running? Okay, very nice!" The voice urges, "Holy shit, let's go, let's go!" Arno confirms, "I'm good, I'm good," and when asked "How's your run?" he replies, "Good." After a final "Jesus" and "Ciao, ciao. Good luck," Arno mentions "Three kilometers" before a "Bye bye." The video concludes with a title card reading 'TRIATHLON PT. 3 16KM - RUNNING' against the volcanic terrain, as the electronic music continues. Strava overlays throughout the segment show details like 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 121' and 'Afternoon Ride' for 'Arno The Kid'.

The video opens with a close-up of the Man in white t-shirt (mustache), wearing a pink and green triathlon suit, orange-tinted sunglasses, and a black cap, running along a paved path. He speaks directly to the camera with a clear, slightly accented voice, expressing satisfaction about finishing the first loop, feeling good and not exhausted, and determined to maintain his pace. The environment is bright and sunny, with a clear blue sky, scattered clouds, and a dry, rocky landscape featuring sparse vegetation and palm trees. He gestures animatedly, at one point raising his fist in excitement, and later turns to face the camera with a wide smile. As he runs, the camera occasionally shifts to show the road and surrounding terrain, including a white wall, a parking lot, and distant hills. He exclaims "Look at that badass!" followed by laughter, as the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) appears, running alongside him. The Man in white t-shirt (curly hair), with a distinct voice, asks "How are you feeling?", to which the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) replies "Good, and you?", and the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) responds "Amazing!", reinforcing their energetic interaction with a high-five and a moment of celebration. The Man in white t-shirt (mustache) continues, explaining that the beginning was "a bit stiff" from the bike and swimming, causing sore arms, and recalls a 3km run from yesterday. He then mentions seeing "Arno" looking "super fresh," expressing pride and confidence in him for the 16k. He humorously admits being "scared to fart" due to potential "wet fart," followed by laughter, then states "the rest, amazing." He notes "good tempo, good heartbeat," and again humorously mentions needing to fart but refraining. He states he still needs to complete "one loop" and expresses extreme dehydration, asking "Can I get a bit of water?" to which the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) readily agrees, "Yeah, sure." Sounds of gasping, gulping, and water pouring are heard as he drinks, commenting "It's warm, eh?" with a sigh of relief. The Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) then asks "Do you have water?", and the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) confirms, offering it: "Yeah, yeah, yeah. Take it." More sounds of rustling, water pouring, and gulping suggest continued hydration. He continues running, occasionally glancing at his watch, his determined expression and wind blowing through his curly hair captured against a coastal view with the ocean, a roundabout, and a parking area with white buildings. The overall atmosphere is energetic and focused, capturing the intensity and joy of a long-distance run in a scenic, arid environment.

event_blocks.summary: Arno, the Man in white t-shirt (mustache), runs along a gravel path in a dry, rocky desert landscape under a clear blue sky, wearing his pink and green triathlon suit, remarking, "It's so weird. I'm not kaput," and confirming, "Hey, it is my last lap, eh?" He expresses exertion with "Yes, it's coming," before slowing to a walk on a paved area near Club La Santa, acknowledging the distance, "16," and his fatigue, "Ah, well done, man. Oh, the legs," finally declaring, "Voilà, that's done. Easy." An on-screen Strava overlay appears, showing 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 121' with 16.00 km and a 5:31/km pace, noting he set his 3rd fastest time in the 15k. He adjusts his red-tinted sunglasses and is greeted by the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair), also in a triathlon suit, who comments, "Light work," and encourages him, "Let's go, man. Come on. 200 more. Come on, Arno!" They share a high-five in the parking lot, where another Strava overlay for 'Arno The Kid' shows 16.04 km and a 6:43/km pace. The two men stand together, catching their breath, removing their sunglasses, and conversing casually in front of the Club La Santa building, discussing needing the toilet and Arno stating, "My muscles... muscles are done," with his companion agreeing, "Muscles, yeah, for me too." They celebrate their accomplishment with exclamations like, "We fucking did it! We did it, motherfucker!" and conclude with "Let's get a shower."

event_blocks.summary: On a rooftop terrace under a bright blue sky, the Man in white t-shirt (mustache), wearing a pink and green triathlon suit, converses with the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) about taking a cold shower and enjoying a chill pool. The scene shifts to a wide shot of them walking by a swimming pool with white buildings and palm trees under a golden-hued sky, before they sit on a concrete ledge. The Man with curly hair holds a Corona Extra, and the Man with the mustache holds a dark beer. They clink bottles, and the Man with the mustache toasts to a productive week and expresses pride in both of them, specifically Milan, who reciprocates the sentiment. The Man with the mustache adjusts his white sock and points to a 'haha' tattoo on his knee, which Milan finds disgusting. The Man with the mustache then excitedly discusses the 'Iron Man project,' calling it emotional and one of the best he's done, noting his improved physical condition and health. He reiterates his pride in Milan's progress and how Milan consistently surprises him after training. Later, the Man in white t-shirt (mustache), now in a 'Stop Working Start Drinking' t-shirt, black shorts, and white socks, enthusiastically praises Milan's performance in a quarter triathlon. Milan, in a black 'HUF' t-shirt and black shorts, admits he underestimated the challenge. The Man with the mustache promotes their upcoming 'Iron Man episode,' calling viewer comments their fuel and urging viewers to like and subscribe for the next video. A 'poof' sound effect is acknowledged as 'Poof notifications squad,' and Milan says, 'Let's go.' Pleasant background music and a serene, tropical ambiance with lush greenery, palm trees, and distant white buildings accompany their friendly banter.

They clink bottles, and the Man with the mustache toasts, saying "Santé, to a very productive week. I'm very proud of us." He then passionately describes the "Iron Man project" as one of the best he's done, noting, "It's been tough, it's been nice. I've never been this in shape, I've never been this healthy in my life." He reiterates his pride in Milan, saying, "I'm proud of you, of what you're doing," and adds, "You always surprise me when you come back from a training. I'm like, he's gonna be dead because I..." The atmosphere is relaxed and friendly, with warm sunlight casting long shadows and highlighting the vibrant colors of the surrounding plants and architecture, as the camera captures close-ups emphasizing their expressions and the casual, intimate nature of their interaction.

On a sun-drenched rooftop terrace with a panoramic coastal view under a golden-hued sky, two men are seated on a curved concrete ledge, engaged in a relaxed conversation. The Man in white t-shirt (mustache), wearing a 'Stop Working Start Drinking' t-shirt, black shorts, and white socks, speaks with an enthusiastic and engaging tone, gesturing animatedly. He expresses admiration for the other man, saying, "I saw you finish like a quarter triathlon right now. You arrived super fresh, super motivated, super happy and I'm like, this guy's gonna do it." Beside him, the Man in black t-shirt (curly hair), in a black 'HUF' t-shirt and black shorts, listens intently, occasionally responding with hand gestures and a calmer, reflective voice, admitting, "It's been amazing. It's been crazy. I underestimated this challenge way too much." The Man in white t-shirt (mustache) then promotes their upcoming "Iron Man episode," stating, "This is the last time you see us without having done the Iron Man," and expresses gratitude for viewer comments, calling them "our carbs" and "the fuel to our motivation." The Man in black t-shirt (curly hair) humorously interjects, "You're the carbs to my body, you know." The Man in white t-shirt (mustache) gives a thumbs-up to the camera, accompanied by on-screen text 'THUMBS UP SUBSCRIBE', urging viewers to "give it a thumbs up, subscribe because the next video will be the Iron Man video." A distinct "poof" sound effect plays, which the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) acknowledges as "Poof notifications squad," prompting the Man in black t-shirt (curly hair) to comment, "That was a beautiful one," before saying "Let's go." Throughout, a pleasant background music track accompanies their friendly banter, enhancing the serene, tropical ambiance with lush greenery, palm trees, and distant white buildings. Between them, a dark beer bottle and a Corona Extra rest on the ledge.

event_blocks.summary: On a serene rooftop terrace during golden hour, the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair), wearing a black t-shirt with a pink and white 'HUF' logo and black shorts, sits upright and remarks "enjoy the sunset," "the sunset," and "Yeah, and then we have to make our bags." The Man in white t-shirt (mustache), clad in a white t-shirt with 'Stop Working Start Drinking' printed on the front and black shorts with white stripes, reclines with his legs stretched out, a 'haha' tattoo visible on his left knee, responding "Muchos flores," "Yeah, fine," and "Back to Belgium." A Corona Extra and a dark beer bottle rest between them on the concrete ledge. The Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) then notes, "Oh, no wind." An upbeat, slightly melancholic instrumental track begins to play. The background features modern architecture with white curved walls, palm trees, succulents, and a distant view of hills and buildings under a soft, pastel sky. The camera pans right, revealing more of the terrace, a white van parked near a pool, and additional white buildings, before transitioning to a white screen displaying bold red 'SUBSCRIBE' text and smaller black text 'JOIN THE FAMILY.' A gray tabby cat appears below the text, sitting and looking around curiously, occasionally tilting its head and shifting its gaze, as the music continues.
